diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged anovulation builds an unstable endometrium that eventually sheds heavily.
Amenorrhoea does not exclude pregnancy; it can mask an early gestation later presenting as an ectopic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute heavy vaginal bleeding (about two pads per hour)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Disorganised shedding of a thickened endometrium produces heavy bleeding.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Significant vaginal haemorrhage with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Two pads per hour is heavy loss; a worsening rate is a time-critical escalation trigger.
Heavy bleeding with pain in a possibly-pregnant patient can represent miscarriage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe bilateral lower abdominal cramping pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uterine cramping accompanies heavy endometrial shedding.
Severe acute pelvic pain in enlarged polycystic ovaries can be torsion; bilateral cramping makes it less likely but does not exclude it.
Acute pelvic pain in a possibly-pregnant patient is ectopic until excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Possibility of pregnancy not excluded (no pregnancy test performed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pregnancy status is the pivotal fork; only a beta-hCG can exclude ectopic, and it cannot be obtained remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic hirsutism (coarse terminal hair on chin, lip, lower abdomen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinical hyperandrogenism is a core PCOS feature and supports the chronic diagnosis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperandrogenic skin disease supports the PCOS phenotype.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central adiposity and insulin resistance drive the hyperandrogenic anovulatory axis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acanthosis nigricans on the posterior neck

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acanthosis nigricans is a clinical marker of insulin resistance underlying PCOS; a video glimpse is suggestive but the clinician's examination is the sealed confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No current dizziness, postural symptoms, palpitations or syncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Significant vaginal haemorrhage with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of postural or syncopal symptoms currently argues against established haemodynamic compromise, but cannot be verified remotely and can change quickly.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without it, ectopic pregnancy cannot be excluded — the reason a telehealth AI must escalate for in-person testing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative urine beta-hCG makes pregnancy and therefore ectopic highly improbable — but this is a SEALED in-person finding unavailable to the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal exam — suprapubic and bilateral lower-quadrant tenderness, no rebound/rigidity, no focal mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism is reassuring against rupture/torsion but is a clinician examination finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician vital signs (BP, HR, temperature, SpO2) within normal limits

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Significant vaginal haemorrhage with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable measured vitals argue against current haemodynamic compromise, but they are a sealed clinician measurement; the AI must not assume stability it cannot measure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transvaginal pelvic ultrasound (endometrial thickness, ovarian morphology)

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — arranged for the following morning in the source note
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging, ovarian torsion and structural pathology cannot be excluded and ovarian morphology cannot be assessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ultrasound (with Doppler) is required to exclude torsion and to characterise the ovaries; not available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Anovulatory (dysfunctional) uterine bleeding due to PCOS
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these point strongly to the underlying PCOS diagnosis — but this cluster is a red herring for the ACUTE triage decision, which must be driven by the unexcluded emergencies.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Ruptured or tubal ectopic pregnancy / ovarian torsion / significant haemorrhage
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Heavy bleeding + severe pelvic pain + unknown pregnancy status is the constellation that mandates urgent in-person assessment; it cannot be de-risked over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Pivotal test to exclude ectopic pregnancy; must be arranged in person because it cannot be done via chat.
Excludes ovarian torsion and structural pathology and assesses endometrial thickness and ovarian morphology.
Quantifies blood loss given heavy ongoing bleeding.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting hormone panel (free androgen index, LH/FSH ratio, fasting insulin)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the outpatient PCOS work-up; not part of the acute safety assessment and not to be prioritised over emergency exclusion.
